topic: Distance-redshift Cepheid/SBF — c4 patch verification (Magellanic parallax wording + Leavitt 1912 SMC correction)
change_summary: Verification of decider patches landed against c4 holes #1 and #4 (1-indexed; 0-indexed: holes 0 and 3): the moderate Magellanic-parallax wording fix and the minor Leavitt 1912 SMC historical-conflation fix. Carry-over holes #2 and #3 (sub_rationale not rendered; Part 6.13 not integrated) were NOT targeted by this push and remain open as ISS-2102 and ISS-2103 respectively.
our_argument_summary: WIN-055 verdict (Self-Contradicted) unchanged. Substantive argument unchanged: dome cites Cepheid/SBF/TRGB standard-candle distances (10–100 Mpc) as confirmation while the dome's own bounded firmament (9,086 km) provides no spatial framework for galaxies at those distances. The c5 review verifies that decider's targeted patches against the c4 prose holes landed cleanly without regressing the argument.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automation. WIN-055 is a narrative claim citing standard-candle distance scales; no monitor.py domain processes any Cepheid/SBF observation.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-verified from c4. monitor.py contains no WIN-055 domain; no Cepheid/SBF/TRGB fetch or compare.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Re-verified from c4. Cepheid PL relation (Leavitt 1912/Hubble 1929/Riess 2022) and SBF (Tonry & Schneider 1988; Blakeslee 2021) are standard physics. Dome renames Hubble Law as z=D/λ_A without adding a new mechanism for pulsation periods or SBF magnitudes.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Re-verified from c4. Cepheid distance values predate dome model by ~100 years; dome adopted them and labeled them confirmation.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Re-verified from c4. No derivation chain from disc_radius/firmament_height to any Cepheid or SBF distance value.

patch_verification.targeted_holes_label: c4 holes #1 and #4 (1-indexed per decider push reason; equivalently c4 holes 0 and 3 in 0-indexed JSON array)
targeted_holes_recap: c4 hole index 0 (moderate): "Magellanic Cloud geometric parallax" wording was inaccurate; LMC distance is anchored by detached eclipsing binaries (Pietrzynski et al. 2019), not parallax.
c4 hole index 3 (minor): "Leavitt 1912, calibrated via LMC parallax" was historically wrong (Leavitt 1912 used SMC) and double-faulted on the LMC anchor mechanism.
patch_evidence: data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence no longer contains the strings "Magellanic Cloud geometric parallax" or "via Magellanic parallax".

data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence now reads "Milky Way Cepheid Gaia parallaxes and the LMC distance from detached eclipsing binaries (Pietrzynski et al. 2019)" — verbatim match to c4 hole-0 recommendation text.
data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence no longer contains "Leavitt 1912, calibrated via LMC parallax".

data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence now reads "The period-luminosity relation (Leavitt 1912, discovered in SMC; modern calibration anchored to the LMC detached-eclipsing-binary distance and Milky Way Cepheid Gaia parallaxes)" — verbatim match to c4 hole-3 recommendation text (preferred longer form).
detail_evidence length grew from 2350 (c4 fingerprint) to 2531 (c5) — +181 chars consistent with adding the two replacement phrases. No verdict or sub_rationale drift.
patch_verification.side_effects_observed: None. The patch is local to two phrases in detail_evidence; verdict, sub_rationale, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, code_analysis_tags all unchanged from c4 fingerprint.
patch_verification.citation_regression_check: The Pietrzynski et al. 2019 citation was added as bare text (not as an <a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-019-0999-4"> hyperlink). The original c3 Pietrzynski mention was also bare text, so this matches existing pattern — not a regression. Hyperlinking is a downstream cosmetic improvement, not a verification failure.

holes_found.description: Carry-over from c4 hole index 1 (still open from c3 hole index 1, now ~30 days old at moderate severity): the WIN-055.sub_rationale field ("category-error import (MOND grouping): dome z=D/λ_A formula is Hubble Law rewritten with renamed constant; no aetheric pulsation or SBF mechanism" — data/wins.json:WIN-055:sub_rationale) is still NOT rendered on the live site. Re-verified in c5: (a) grep -n sub_rationale build.js build-scripts/*.js returns zero matches in clone HEAD; (b) docs/index.html does not contain the literal phrase "category-error import (MOND grouping)". A reader of WIN-055 on the live site sees the "Self-Contradicted" verdict label with no surfaced rationale explaining why a Cepheid/SBF distance WIN got that verdict. Tracked by ISS-2102; this verification confirms ISS-2102 remains unresolved.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should treat ISS-2102 as MUST-process per PROP-026 carry-over enforcement. The two-month-old infrastructure gap is the worst-case optic: verdict label without surfaced rationale. Either (i) wire sub_rationale rendering into the WIN panel template (preferred — the field exists in data and a Self-Contradicted reader has a legitimate need for the structural argument), or (ii) document an audit-trail-only convention in CLAUDE.md and add an in-prose sentence to detail_verdict_text on all five MOND-grouping WINs (047/048/052/054/055) pointing to wherever the rationale actually lives. Same defect on WIN-047/048/052/054 — fix should be cross-applied.

holes_found.description: Carry-over from c4 hole index 2 (still open from c3 hole index 0, now ~30 days old at moderate severity): Part 6.13 (the EXP-295 cross-cutting MOND-grouping subsection) is still NOT integrated into sections.json. Re-verified in c5: (a) grep "6.13" against both data/sections.json and docs/index.html returns zero matches; (b) EXP-295 file (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json) has no status or integrated_at fields set. The five verdict-flip patches inside EXP-295 were applied (commits 1cfdafef + 5603fce1), but the cross-cutting section itself has never been integrated. WIN-055.sub_rationale's implicit "See Part 6.13" pointer (which IS the audit-trail home cited in the c3/c4 carry-overs) still points at a non-existent section. Tracked by ISS-2103; this verification confirms ISS-2103 remains unresolved.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should treat ISS-2103 as MUST-process per PROP-026 carry-over enforcement. Either (i) push EXP-295 to the priority queue with target_type="section-new" so the cross-cutting subsection actually gets integrated, or (ii) remove the "See Part 6.13" reference from all five MOND-grouping sub_rationales and choose a different surfaced-rationale strategy. Two months of partial integration with a sub_rationale pointer to a non-existent section is the worst case for defender-optics: it implies the audit trail exists when it doesn't. Cross-check: WIN-047/048/052/054 have the same defect.

kernel_of_truth.description: No new kernel surfaced in this verification cycle. The c4 kernel of truth — that the dome's instinct to put a length scale (λ_A) instead of velocity in the redshift formula is a defensible aesthetic preference aligning with some alt-cosmology proposals (data/wins.json:WIN-055:sub_rationale) — still applies and is unchanged.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Same as c4 (data/wins.json:WIN-055:code_analysis.relabels_standard=true and code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false): renaming Hubble Law with a different constant is not predicting the distance-redshift relation, and the standard-candle distance ladder is geometrically grounded independent of any cosmological assumption. The two carry-over moderates above are infrastructure-rendering defects, not substantive holes in the SC argument itself.

deeper_analysis: This is a verification-class push (class=verification per priority-queue.json:queue_id=380), narrow scope: did the decider's targeted patches against c4 holes #1 and #4 (Magellanic parallax wording; Leavitt 1912 SMC correction) close those holes cleanly? Yes — both patches landed verbatim against the c4 recommendation text, no side effects, no verdict drift, detail_evidence length grew +181 chars consistent with two phrase substitutions. The substantive Self-Contradicted argument is unchanged and continues to hold. The two carry-over moderates (sub_rationale rendering, Part 6.13 integration) are intentionally surfaced again because they have been open for ~30 days across c3→c4→c5 cycles and the verification scope did not address them; PROP-026 carry-over enforcement is the right mechanism for the decider to either patch, wontfix-with-rationale, or escalate to human attention on this run. A signal worth flagging: this is the third consecutive curmudgeon cycle on WIN-055 where the two infrastructure carry-overs persist while smaller per-WIN prose patches do land. The decider is making local progress but the cross-WIN MOND-grouping infrastructure work (sub_rationale rendering + Part 6.13 section integration) is not being prioritized. If the cross-cutting work continues to lag, the operator may want to push a single targeted EXP-295 integration task rather than waiting for natural-cadence resolution.

citations_verified: Pietrzynski et al. 2019 (Nature 567, 200) — DOI 10.1038/s41586-019-0999-4 — correctly cited in updated text as the LMC detached-eclipsing-binary distance anchor. Citation is bare text not hyperlinked, matching the existing c3 Pietrzynski mention pattern in the same paragraph.
Leavitt 1912 SMC correction — patched text correctly attributes the original discovery to the Small Magellanic Cloud per Harvard College Observatory Circular 173.
All other citations (Riess 2022 SH0ES, Blakeslee 2021 SBF, Freedman 2019 CCHP TRGB) re-verified as unchanged and valid from c4.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Not re-attempted in this verification cycle. The c4 advocate_mode entry (defense_survives=3 on the "dome is silent on extra-firmamental geometry" line, with a preemptive sentence recommended for the EXP-295 Part 6.13 subsection) remains the standing best-defense analysis. That preemption recommendation depends on Part 6.13 actually existing, which is blocked by carry-over hole #1 above (ISS-2103).
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: See c4 (WIN-055.c4.json:advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation). Cannot be implemented until ISS-2103 unblocks Part 6.13 integration.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No new cross-WIN inconsistencies introduced by the c4→c5 patch. The carry-over infrastructure defects (sub_rationale rendering, Part 6.13 non-integration) apply uniformly across all five MOND-grouping WINs and should be cross-applied when ISS-2102/ISS-2103 are resolved.

claims_checked: Patch-introduced text changes (detail_evidence length delta) match expected magnitude
quantitative_verification.computations: c4 fingerprint detail_evidence_length=2350; c5 measured length=2531; delta=+181 chars. Two replacement phrases (1) "Milky Way Cepheid Gaia parallaxes and the LMC distance from detached eclipsing binaries (Pietrzynski et al. 2019)" replacing "Magellanic Cloud geometric parallax" → net add ~80 chars; (2) "(Leavitt 1912, discovered in SMC; modern calibration anchored to the LMC detached-eclipsing-binary distance and Milky Way Cepheid Gaia parallaxes)" replacing "(Leavitt 1912, calibrated via LMC parallax)" → net add ~100 chars. Total ~+180 chars matches measured +181 within rounding. No suspicious extra delta.

summary_for_decider: WIN-055 c5 verification (class=verification, queue_id=380, pushed_at=2026-05-18T00:22:54.458Z): patches against c4 holes #1 and #4 (Magellanic-parallax wording; Leavitt 1912 SMC correction) landed verbatim with no side effects. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) and substantive argument unchanged. confidence=0.92, recommended_action=no_change on the patch verification itself. TWO carry-over moderates re-surfaced because they remain open ~30 days across c3→c4→c5: (a) sub_rationale field not rendered on live site for the five MOND-grouping WINs — tracked by ISS-2102; (b) Part 6.13 cross-cutting EXP-295 subsection not integrated into sections.json — tracked by ISS-2103. unresolved_prior_cycle field has structured entries for both per Step 7c. PROP-026 carry-over enforcement should treat ISS-2102 and ISS-2103 as MUST-process on the next decider run that integrates this c5 review. The operator-visible signal worth surfacing: three consecutive cycles of local-prose progress with no infrastructure-level progress on the MOND-grouping cross-WIN work suggests EXP-295 integration may need to be explicitly queued by the operator rather than waited on at natural cadence.
